title: ScrollArea Component
content:
The `ScrollArea` component provides a customizable and accessible scrolling container, built on top of Radix UI's `ScrollArea` primitive. It allows for elegant overflow management with distinct visual styling for scrollbars. ## Key Exports ### `ScrollArea` A primary component that wraps the `ScrollAreaPrimitive.Root`. It accepts standard `ScrollAreaPrimitive.Root` props and applies default styling for overflow and rounding. It also includes the `ScrollBar` component and `ScrollAreaPrimitive.Corner` for a complete scrolling experience. **Props:** - `className`: Custom CSS classes to apply to the root element. - `children`: The content to be displayed within the scrollable area. - `...props`: Accepts any valid `React.ComponentProps`. ### `ScrollBar` A component responsible for rendering the scrollbar. It can be oriented vertically or horizontally and features a customizable thumb. **Props:** - `className`: Custom CSS classes to apply to the scrollbar element. - `orientation`: ('vertical' | 'horizontal') Determines the orientation of the scrollbar. Defaults to 'vertical'. - `...props`: Accepts any valid `React.ComponentProps`. ## Usage Example ```jsx import { ScrollArea } from '@/components/ui/scroll-area'; function MyComponent() { return (
{/* Your scrollable content goes here */}

This is some content that will overflow.

Add enough lines to trigger the scrollbar.

More content...

Even more content...

And so on.

Just a bit more to ensure scrolling.

Almost there...

Final line of content.
); } ``` This example demonstrates a basic `ScrollArea` usage. The `ScrollArea` component will automatically manage the overflow of its children and display scrollbars when necessary. You can further customize the appearance by passing `className` props to both `ScrollArea` and `ScrollBar`.
